## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-25 01:39:46 PT

Found 0 violations across 4 files. All changes are compliant with tripwire rules.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered

Based on the changed files, the following category tripwires were loaded:
- **Documentation tripwires** (docs/learned/documentation/tripwires.md) - for documentation changes
- **Testing tripwires** (docs/learned/testing/tripwires.md) - for test file changes
- **Architecture tripwires** (docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md) - for gateway code changes

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical)

✅ `subprocess\.run\(` - None found
✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found
✅ `os\.chdir\(` - None found
✅ `\bimport time\b|time\.sleep\(|datetime\.now\(` - None found
✅ `@patch|monkeypatch\.` - None found
✅ `\bgt\s+(sync|submit|restack|create|modify)` - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches

✅ **Adding code blocks to learned docs** - No code blocks added, only prose descriptions updated
✅ **Copying source code verbatim** - No verbatim code copies detected
✅ **Using line numbers in source pointers** - Not applicable, no source pointer changes
✅ **Test assertion updates after behavior changes** - Test assertions correctly updated to match new pancake indicator behavior
✅ **Modifying business logic without adding tests** - Tests were updated alongside code changes
✅ **Using bare subprocess.run** - None found
✅ **Adding file I/O to __init__** - None found
✅ **Raising exceptions for expected failures** - No exception patterns changed

### Files Reviewed

- `docs/learned/desktop-dash/visual-status-indicators.md`: 0 violations
- `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md`: 0 violations  
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/unit/plan_store/test_lifecycle_display.py`: 0 violations

### Analysis

This PR correctly re-introduces the pancake emoji (🥞) for stacked PR indication and updates the blocking logic to use an explicit non-blocking set (`{"🥞", "👀", "✔"}`) rather than a single exclusion check. The changes are internally consistent:

1. **Code change** properly implements the non-blocking indicator set pattern
2. **Test updates** correctly update all assertions to expect the pancake emoji in output
3. **Documentation updates** accurately describe the new implementation using prose (not verbatim code)

All existing review comments on this PR have been resolved in previous commits.

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-25 01:39:46 PT**: No violations detected. PR re-introduces stacked PR indicator with proper non-blocking classification. 4 files reviewed (docs, tests, lifecycle logic). All tripwire checks passed.
- **2026-02-24 21:39:58 PT**: No violations detected. PR re-introduces stacked PR indicator with proper non-blocking classification. 4 files reviewed (docs, tests, lifecycle logic). All tripwire checks passed.
- **2026-02-24 21:30:31 PT**: No violations detected. PR re-introduces stacked PR indicator with proper non-blocking classification. 4 files reviewed (docs, tests, lifecycle logic). All tripwire checks passed.

</details>

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!-- erk:pr-address-run -->
## PR Review Comments Addressed

This PR was updated by an automated process triggered by:
```
erk pr address-remote 7974
```

### What is this?

[Erk](https://github.com/schrockn/erk) is a CLI tool for plan-oriented agentic
engineering. The `pr-address` workflow uses Claude to read PR review comments
and address them automatically.

### Changes Made

The PR review addressed inaccuracies in documentation about plan backend selection logic and stacked PR indicators:

• **Environment Variable Isolation**: Corrected the description of how `context_for_test()` selects the plan backend — it uses explicit conditional logic that defaults to `PlannedPRBackend` rather than a "tautological comparison," and clarified that the `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` environment variable is never read.

• **Stacked PR Indicator**: Updated documentation to reflect that the pancake emoji indicator is detected but not actually added to the display by `_build_indicators()` — the `is_stacked` parameter is accepted but currently unused, making it a no-op rather than an active feature.

### Details

- **Model:** claude-opus-4-6
- **Workflow run:** [View details](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22380899822)
- **Commits added:** 1

---
*Automated by erk pr-address workflow*
